title: Multidisciplinary training for professionals in the social and educational system of the Federal  District – Brazil
author: Tayane Medeiros de Oliveira
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: In the contemporary toward permanent education in health, has built up a training course for professionals in the socioeducational system of the Federal District in order to exchange knowledge related to socioeducation, adolescence, alcohol, other drugs and multidisciplinary. mixed methodology with qualitative and quantitative approach, focused on the process of learning and acting with teen agers through comprehensive care of a multidisciplinary team. From the course of the application protocol, it was possible to analyze the situational overview of the system's qualification of the professionals. From 92 professionals, it was found that 90.2% (83) claimed to have made improvement course and 56.7% (51) training about alcohol and other drugs. However, 86.7% (78) treat adolescents who used / abused of substances and, surprisingly, 85.4% (70) do not feel able to perform this service. This datas shows the low quality of training and, consequently, low quality care. The datas demonstrates that the quality of training offered to professionals is low, as the frequency of these capabilities. That increases the system's fragility and reducing quality of care.
